mechanism: G7 fiscal divergence affects global demand for oil, while OPEC+ fiscal breakevens constrain the supply response. Continued G7 fiscal expansion sustains demand above breakeven levels, maintaining OPEC+ cohesion; consolidation threatens it.
regime_dependence: Strongest when oil prices are near the aggregate breakeven ($78-85/bbl). Above $90, fiscal concerns are secondary; below $70, fiscal stress dominates regardless of G7 policy.
